heading: Political activity authorized; prohibitions

children.text: use his official authority or influence for the purpose of interfering with or affecting the result of an election;

children.label: A
children.text: a member of the same Federal labor organization as defined under or a Federal employee organization which as of the date of enactment of the Hatch Act Reform Amendments of 1993 had a multicandidate political committee (as defined under section 315(a)(4) of the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 ());section 7103(4) of this title2 U.S.C. 441a(a)(4)1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: not a subordinate employee; and

children.label: C
children.text: the solicitation is for a contribution to the multicandidate political committee (as defined under section 315(a)(4) of the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 ()) of such Federal labor organization as defined under or a Federal employee organization which as of the date of the enactment of the Hatch Act Reform Amendments of 1993 had a multicandidate political committee (as defined under section 315(a)(4) of the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 ()); or2 U.S.C. 441a(a)(4)section 7103(4) of this title2 U.S.C. 441a(a)(4)11
run for the nomination or as a candidate for election to a partisan political office; or

children.label: A
children.text: has an application for any compensation, grant, contract, ruling, license, permit, or certificate pending before the employing office of such employee; or

children.label: B
children.text: is the subject of or a participant in an ongoing audit, investigation, or enforcement action being carried out by the employing office of such employee.

An employee of the Federal Election Commission (except one appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate), may not request or receive from, or give to, an employee, a Member of Congress, or an officer of a uniformed service a political contribution.

children.label: A
children.text: No employee described under subparagraph (B) (except one appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate), may take an active part in political management or political campaigns.

children.label: I
children.text: the Federal Election Commission or the Election Assistance Commission;

children.label: II
children.text: the Federal Bureau of Investigation;

children.label: III
children.text: the Secret Service;

children.label: IV
children.text: the Central Intelligence Agency;

children.label: V
children.text: the National Security Council;

children.label: VI
children.text: the National Security Agency;

children.label: VII
children.text: the Defense Intelligence Agency;

children.label: VIII
children.text: the Merit Systems Protection Board;

children.label: IX
children.text: the Office of Special Counsel;

children.label: X
children.text: the Office of Criminal Investigation of the Internal Revenue Service;

children.label: XI
children.text: the Office of Investigative Programs of the United States Customs Service;

children.label: XII
children.text: the Office of Law Enforcement of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, and Firearms;

children.label: XIII
children.text: the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency; or

children.label: XIV
children.text: the Office of the Director of National Intelligence; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a person employed in a position described under section 3132(a)(4), 5372, 5372a, or 5372b of title 5, United States Code.

No employee of the Criminal Division or National Security Division of the Department of Justice (except one appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate), may take an active part in political management or political campaigns.

For purposes of this subsection, the term “active part in political management or in a political campaign” means those acts of political management or political campaigning which were prohibited for employees of the competitive service before , by determinations of the Civil Service Commission under the rules prescribed by the President.July 19, 1940

children.label: c
children.text: An employee retains the right to vote as he chooses and to express his opinion on political subjects and candidates.
